content.title: The Whisper
content.subtitle: Origin: The Static · The Ones Between

entries.label: Origin
entries.value: The Static — a dimension of shifting frequencies and recursive signal patterns

entries.label: Appearance
entries.value: Human-passing. Proportions slightly wrong. Solid-color eyes. Shadow doesn't always match.

entries.label: Build
entries.value: Humanoid, unsettlingly close to human — uncanny valley

content.label: Dimensional Perception
content.text: Analyze is free and automatic against rift phenomena — you read active weaves, banked rounds, pattern signatures, and dimensional instability on sight. When a Null Zone or rift hazard would surprise the party, it never surprises you; the GM owes you the warning. (Analyze — see Turn, AP & Reactions.)
The Adjustment (Active, 1 AP): Once per fight, shift yourself or a willing adjacent ally one band, no triggers. Reality, locally renegotiated.

content.label: The Wrongness
content.text: First-impression social Checks at disadvantage with strangers. Crews acclimate; lobbies don't.

content.label: Never on the Books
content.text: You cannot hold corp licenses in your own name — all licensed goods (patterns, specialty ammo, exotic permits) cost +20% through intermediaries. The rift-specialist race pays retail-plus for rift tools, forever.

content.label: PAPERWORK
content.text: You don't scan correctly. Every checkpoint is a coin flip between waved-through and detained-for-study.

content.label: Strengths
items: Irreplaceable utility — their perception is something no technology can replicate
Never classified as specimens — even corporations were afraid to cage them
The Whisper Network — information sharing mechanism that no corp can tap or regulate

content.label: Weaknesses
items: Can't explain what they perceive — their warnings are accurate but untranslatable
No unified culture — no community support structure, perpetually isolated
Unsettling presence — even allies never fully relax around them
Shadow doesn't match — subtle wrongness that people notice subconsciously

content.text: Human-passing at a glance. Proportions slightly wrong — too symmetrical, too smooth. Solid-color eyes (no iris/pupil distinction). Shadow doesn't always match body position. Dimensionally attuned — perceive rift energy the way humans perceive light. Their perception includes layers of reality that human language can't describe. Localized dimensional manipulation — minor reality adjustments in their immediate vicinity.

Appeared in the 2210s — not arriving through rifts, just suddenly being there. No rift event correlated with their appearance. No corporation imported them. They were never classified as specimens — the unspoken reason is fear. Nobody wanted to find out what happened when you put a Whisper in a cage. The Whisper Network — named by human intelligence analysts, not by Whispers. Information sharing mechanism that operates through dimensional perception (possibly). Pear has spent 18 classified reports trying to understand it. Sentinel has a 140-year-old uncollected bounty for anyone who can explain it.
Whispers don't share their history because it doesn't translate. Their memory of the last 800 years includes events in dimensional layers that no human instrument detected.

Treated well because irreplaceable. Pear and Soma value their dimensional perception for rift monitoring. Corps can't tap, regulate, or understand the Whisper Network. Wary respect rather than exploitation — "useful and terrifying" is a better employment category than "expendable."

Unsettling. Useful. When the Whisper says "don't go that way," everyone listens — the last time someone didn't listen, they walked into a Null Zone. They don't explain their warnings. The explanations don't translate. Crews that work with Whispers long enough learn to trust the instinct without needing the rationale.

content.label: Roleplay Hooks
items: You see layers of reality that nobody else can perceive. How do you handle knowing things you can't explain?
People are uncomfortable around you. Always. How do you build relationships despite the uncanny valley?
You were never caged. The reason might be worse than caging. Do you know why?
Your shadow doesn't always match. When did you first notice? When did someone else?

content.text: When the Whisper says don't go that way — don't go that way.
